content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DIRECTION title=Graph DB should not be the default starting point for memory layer summary=The architecture direction is to start with relational/document-vector primitives because `Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` already provide graph-like semantics; introduce a Graph database only when traversal/path queries become a product requirement. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 17. [1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Baseline memory-layer stack before Graph DB summary=For the memory layer, the recommended sufficient baseline stack is Postgres, JSONB, pgvector, full-text indexes, and lineage tables, relying on existing model links for graph-like behavior. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6> keywords=Postgres,JSONB,pgvector,full-text indexes,lineage tables tags=doc_digest,baseline_stack,memory_infrastructure evidence=Для memory layer обычно хватает: - Postgres - JSONB - pgvector - full-text indexes - lineage tables

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`v3` memory architecture uses four axes to avoid brittle or dump-like memory summary=In `v3`, memory is organized along four axes—Representation (Source/Claim/Note/Profile/Task/Episode), Lifecycle (write→reconcile→consolidate→repair→retain/archive/delete), Retrieval policy (claim-first/note-first/mixed/evidence-first with bounded budgets), and Scope & lineage (scope_json/scope_text/source refs/origin note/memory runs). The document states all four are necessary to prevent transcri...[truncated 44 chars] scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=v3,four axes,representation lifecycle retrieval scope lineage tags=v3,architecture,memory_model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended pipeline routes routed writes through canonicalization, specialized updaters, reconciliation, and storage summary=In the recommended architecture, Memory Router outcomes (noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed) feed Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer, then specialized components (Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, Note Constructor) and reconcilers write to Postgres + pgvector, with background consolidation/repair/retention workers and runtime retrieval-answer loop. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory Router,Entity Canonicalizer,Postgres + pgvector,Read-Time Retrieval Planner tags=pipeline,recommended_architecture,component_roles useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture summary=Section 5 defines five storage tiers with distinct roles: Tier A for compact in-context prompt blocks, Tier B for typed long-term records, Tier C for semantic note-layer context, Tier D for immutable raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes across memory objects. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Storage tiers,Tier A,Tier B,Tier C,Tier D,Tier E tags=memory_architecture,doc_digest,storage_tiers useCount=0

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
